title: Balance transfer reads then debits without row-level lock — concurrent transfers double-spend funds
exploit: Two concurrent transfer(A, B, 100) calls both pass the balance >= 100 check before either UPDATE executes; both decrement the balance, spending 200 from an account that only had 100
fix: Wrap both UPDATEs in a transaction and precede the SELECT with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE to hold a row lock for the duration

title: Rate limiter reads counter then increments non-atomically — burst requests bypass the limit
exploit: 10 concurrent login requests all read count=4 before any UPDATE fires; all pass the count<5 check and proceed; the limit is bypassed with no real enforcement
fix: Use an atomic DB increment-and-return (e.g. UPDATE rate_limits SET count = count + 1 WHERE user_id = ? AND count < 5; check affected rows = 1) or a Redis INCR with EXPIRE

title: Token validated then session inserted non-atomically — concurrent deactivation creates session for inactive account
exploit: Thread A validates token and passes the active=1 check; admin concurrently deactivates the user; Thread A then inserts a session for a deactivated account, granting authenticated access
fix: Check active status inside the same transaction as the session INSERT, or re-validate inside the INSERT with a conditional (INSERT INTO sessions ... WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id=? AND active=1))

title: Session row created before active-status validation — orphaned authenticated session on concurrent deactivation
exploit: Session is inserted before the active=1 check; a concurrent deactivation job can arrive after INSERT but before DELETE, and if the DELETE is lost under load, the session row remains valid and grants access to a deactivated account
fix: Move the active check before the INSERT, or use a conditional INSERT (INSERT ... WHERE active = 1) to make it atomic
